channel.title: Higher Ed Intelligence Brief
channel.description: Weekly briefing with links and LinkedIn-ready drafts.

item.title: MA Budget: Community College SUCCESS Fund (7100-4002)
item.description: Anchor page for tracking SUCCESS language/amounts and wraparound support intent through the FY27 budget process.

item.title: U.S. Department of Labor RSS: releases.xml
item.description: Federal workforce-development grants and policy signals frequently originate at DOL/ETA—high relevance for community colleges.

item.title: The Key Podcast: The Pell Shortfall Is a Public Policy ‘Success Story’
item.description: The Key Podcast: The Pell Shortfall Is a Public Policy ‘Success Story’ sara.custer@in… Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM Byline(s) IHE Staff

item.title: Will There Be a Second Compact?
item.description: Will There Be a Second Compact? sara.custer@in… Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM The Department of Education will struggle to find broad acceptance of a new iteration of the Compact for Academic Excellence in Higher Education without true partnership with the sector. Byline(s) Sara Custer

item.title: How RFK Jr. Is Trying to Revamp Medical School
item.description: The health secretary, Robert F. Kennedy Jr., has tapped into an old debate about how much doctors should know about nutrition. But some of his ideas, and tactics, concern medical experts.

item.title: After Pete Hegseth Cut Pentagon Funding, Harvard Offers Options for Military
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has said the Pentagon would end funds supporting active-duty service members at Harvard. The school is offering military students alternatives to defer or go elsewhere.

item.title: Hegseth Cuts Ties With Yale, Georgetown and Other Top Universities
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth accused Yale, Princeton, Brown and other elite colleges and think tanks of indoctrinating service members with liberal ideologies.

item.title: Columbia Student Detained by ICE Promotes ‘Beauty’ and ‘Brains’ Online
item.description: Elmina Aghayeva has 114,000 followers on Instagram and has seemingly never posted about politics, unlike other Columbia University students detained by immigration officers.

item.title: Racist, Antisemitic Slurs Surface in Florida GOP Group Chat
item.description: After the secretary of the Miami-Dade County Republican Party created the chat for college students, it devolved into slurs against Black and Jewish people.

item.title: U.S. Schools Are Betting Big on A.I. Will New York City Be Next?
item.description: This could be a pivotal year in shaping what role artificial intelligence plays in American schools. Some families want Mayor Mamdani to hit the brakes.

item.title: Columbia Is Investigated for Handling of Sex-Abuse Claims Against Doctor
item.description: Attorney General Letitia James is investigating the university for its actions after the 2012 arrest of Robert Hadden, a former Columbia gynecologist convicted of sex crimes.

item.title: Judge Vacates Punishments of Columbia Students Who Occupied a Building
item.description: Pro-Palestinian demonstrators who had taken over Hamilton Hall in 2024 faced expulsions, suspensions and the revocation of their degrees.

item.title: McMahon Touts First Year in Office; Dems and Students Push Back
item.description: McMahon Touts First Year in Office; Dems and Students Push Back jessica.blake@… Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM The Education Department says that in just one year McMahon has secured reforms in higher ed that conservatives have championed for decades. Critics say her work is creating barriers to college access. Byline(s) Jessica Blake Katherine Knott

item.title: Rider University Sells Part of Campus to County in $10M Deal
item.description: Rider University Sells Part of Campus to County in $10M Deal sara.custer@in… Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM Byline(s) Sara Custer

item.title: In-Person Classes Aren’t Safe From the AI Cheating Boom
item.description: In-Person Classes Aren’t Safe From the AI Cheating Boom kathryn.palmer… Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM One professor was surprised to learn how easy digital technology makes it for students to cheat on class participation assignments and some exams. Byline(s) Kathryn Palmer

item.title: How Well Do For-Profit Colleges Serve Black Students?
item.description: How Well Do For-Profit Colleges Serve Black Students? Sara Weissman Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM These colleges enroll higher shares of Black students compared to other institutions, and larger percentages of those students take on debt, according to a new report. Byline(s) Sara Weissman

item.title: Labor Watch: St. John’s Axes Unions, CSU Strike Pays Off
item.description: Labor Watch: St. John’s Axes Unions, CSU Strike Pays Off Emma Whitford Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM In the debut edition of Labor Watch,  Inside Higher Ed recaps major faculty, staff and graduate student labor activity in February.  Byline(s) Emma Whitford

item.title: How One HBCU Is Building a Rural Dental Workforce
item.description: How One HBCU Is Building a Rural Dental Workforce Joshua.Bay Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM Meharry Medical College trains students through mobile clinics to close dental care gaps in underserved communities—including youth in state custody. Byline(s) Joshua Bay

item.title: N.Y. Attorney General’s Office Tells NYU Langone to Resume Youth Trans Care
item.description: N.Y. Attorney General’s Office Tells NYU Langone to Resume Youth Trans Care Ryan Quinn Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM Byline(s) Ryan Quinn

item.title: Extraordinary Higher Education Scholar to Lead World’s Largest Education Research Association
item.description: Extraordinary Higher Education Scholar to Lead World’s Largest Education Research Association Sara Brady Thu, 03/05/2026 - 03:00 AM It is nearly impossible to write anything good or credible about campus climate, diversity and inclusion, sense of belonging, the success of students of color in STEM fields, or Hispanic-serving institutions without citing her studies. Byline(s) Shaun Harper
